site.name: Stillwater Schools Finance Project
site.tagline: Independent research and analysis on the finances of Stillwater Area Public Schools (ISD 834).
site.hero_title: Following the dollars in Stillwater Area Public Schools.
site.hero_lede: A nonpartisan research effort that reads the audits, budgets, levy filings, and state data behind Independent School District No. 834 so residents, journalists, and board members don't have to. We gather the primary documents — five years of audited reports, the state's per-pupil spending data, and Minnesota's school-finance law — reconcile the numbers, and publish plain-language analysis of where the district's money comes from and where it goes.
site.as_of: Figures span the FY2021–FY2025 audited and MN Report Card period and the district's FY2025–26 budget. Enrollment is the October 2025 (FY2026) count.

trends.title: Per-pupil spending: five-year trend
trends.blurb: Total spending per student (General Operating Fund, state, local, and federal) as reported to the Minnesota Report Card. Stillwater has pulled steadily above the statewide average since FY2022.
highlights: Stillwater's per-pupil spending rose 25.7% in four years — from $13,743 (FY2021) to $17,272 (FY2025).
Special-education spending per pupil climbed from $2,576 to $3,558 (+38%) over the same period.
Federal dollars per pupil fell from $546 (FY2021) to $335 (FY2025) as pandemic aid wound down.
trends.source: MN Report Card — Fiscal Transparency, ISD 834, FY2021–FY2025 (data as of April 17, 2026).

collections.title: Analysis & Summaries
collections.blurb: Consolidated, reconciled financial summaries the project has prepared from the primary documents.

collections.title: Source Document Library
collections.blurb: The primary record: five years of audited reports and official budgets, plus the state enrollment, per-pupil, legislative, and audit-standard sources behind the analysis. Grouped by origin.

collections.title: News Coverage
collections.blurb: A compiled, sourced index of a decade of public news about the district — the BOLD school closures, leadership changes, referendums, and budget pressures — with links to primary articles.

collections.title: Reference & Methodology
collections.blurb: Working notes on how Minnesota school finance works, how district financial health is measured, and where the underlying public data lives.
